title: SpaceX Will Not Get Fast-Tracked Entry Into the S&P 500. Here's What That Means for Investors.
author: Patrick Sanders
description: S&P Dow Jones Indices rejected fast-track rules for adding mega-cap IPOs, meaning SpaceX won't enter the S&P 500 until mid-2027 at the earliest. However, the company will be added to the Nasdaq-100 and Russell 1000 much sooner, allowing those index funds to capture the stock quickly. While S&P 500 index funds will miss the initial buying surge, other avenues remain available for investors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Will miss the initial buying surge and index inclusion pop for SpaceX due to S&P 500's delayed entry timeline, reducing near-term trading activity and potential gains.
Will gain SpaceX exposure much sooner through Nasdaq-100 inclusion on the 15th trading day, capturing the initial momentum and index fund buying pressure.
Can include SpaceX as early as the fifth trading day through Russell 1000 inclusion, positioning it to benefit from early index fund buying activity.

title: MEXC Lists 17th Batch of Ondo Tokenized U.S. Assets Spanning Defense, Growth, Value, and Financials
author: Na
description: MEXC has listed four new Ondo tokenized U.S. asset trading pairs (ITAON/USDT, IWFON/USDT, IWNON/USDT, and WFCON/USDT) on its spot market, expanding access to real-world assets including ETFs and stocks. The listing reflects MEXC's evolution toward a broader financial gateway offering both cryptocurrencies and tokenized traditional assets.

title: Are 'Old Economy' Stocks The Real AI Winners? Goldman Flags A Major Market Shift
author: Piero Cingari
description: Goldman Sachs analyst Peter Oppenheimer suggests a structural market shift is underway, with 'old economy' stocks emerging as potential AI winners. As AI infrastructure demands massive capital investment in physical assets like data centers, power grids, and industrial equipment, returns from mega-cap tech stocks are slowing. Value stocks have begun outperforming growth stocks, signaling that the next phase of AI gains may come from traditional sectors rather than Silicon Valley.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Amazon is mentioned as a hyperscaler investing heavily in AI infrastructure, but the article suggests returns from such mega-cap tech investments may be slowing, creating a mixed outlook.
Alphabet is identified as a hyperscaler with massive AI capex, but faces investor scrutiny about ROI adequacy, similar to other mega-cap tech companies experiencing performance slowdown.
Alphabet is identified as a hyperscaler with massive AI capex, but faces investor scrutiny about ROI adequacy, similar to other mega-cap tech companies experiencing performance slowdown.
Meta is listed as a hyperscaler with significant AI spending, but subject to the same concerns about slowing returns and performance deceleration affecting the broader mega-cap tech sector.
Value stocks are highlighted as emerging winners, with IWD outperforming growth ETFs by 18 percentage points over three months, representing the market shift toward traditional sectors.
Growth stocks are underperforming as mega-cap tech returns decelerate, with IWF significantly lagging value stocks, suggesting reduced investor enthusiasm for traditional growth-focused investments.

title: Can Growth ETFs Power Ahead as Optimism Builds?
author: Na
description: The current economic environment presents opportunities for growth-oriented investments, with the S&P 500 Growth Index outperforming value indexes. Factors like softer inflation, positive earnings season, and anticipated Fed rate cuts are fueling market optimism.

description: The article discusses three growth-focused ETFs that investors can consider for long-term investment: Vanguard Growth ETF, Invesco Nasdaq Next Gen 100 ETF, and iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF, each offering unique strategies for capturing market growth potential.
